name: Chocolate-Dipped Orange Shortbread Hearts
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon freshly grated orange zest (from about 2 n)
6 ounces fine-quality bittersweet chocolate (not unsweetened), chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. In a bowl whisk together flour, zest, and salt.
3. In another bowl with an electric mixer beat butter, juice, and confectioners' sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in flour mixture until a dough is just formed.
4. Pat dough into a disk and chill, wrapped in plastic wrap, 30 minutes, or until firm.
5. On a lightly floured surface with a floured rolling pin, roll dough into a 13- by 12-inch rectangle (about 1/3 inch thick).
6. Cut out cookies with a 3-1/2 to 4-inch heart-shaped cutter dipped in flour and arrange 1 inch apart on baking sheets.
7. Using dough scraps, roll and cut out more cookies in the same manner.
8. Bake shortbread in batches in the middle of oven 10 - 15 minutes, or until pale golden, and cool completely on baking sheets on racks.
9. Carefully transfer cookies to a platter.
10. In a double boiler or in a metal bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water melt chocolate, stirring until smooth.
11. Pour warm chocolate onto a plate and dip half of the top side of each cookie, transferring cookies, chocolate sides up, to a wax-paper-lined tray.
12. Chill cookies until chocolate is hardened. Cookies keep chilled, in an airtight container, 4 days. About 12 cookies.
